specification.topic-statement: The LLM request initiation system constructs a user message from typed input, persists it to the database, and starts an asynchronous provider stream that will deliver the model's response.

scope.in-scope: Model presence check, user message creation and in-memory addition, database persistence of the user message, response state clearing, idle flag update, state transition to waiting, lazy provider initialization, LLM request construction, and async stream start.
scope.boundary: What happens as the stream delivers text and tool call events is handled by the LLM response handling subsystem. This topic covers only the initiation of the request.

How each provider constructs HTTP requests, handles authentication, and delivers streaming events is handled by the individual provider implementations. This topic invokes the provider through a standard interface.
How the token cache tracks per-turn token usage is handled by the token cache subsystem. This topic records the start of a new turn.
Whether user input triggers a slash command or an LLM request is decided by the newline action handler. This topic receives only confirmed LLM message text.

behavior.model-check: Before any action, the presence of a configured model is verified. If no model is configured, an error message is appended to the scrollback and the function returns. No message is created, no database record is written, and the agent's state is not changed.
behavior.user-message-creation: A user-role message object is created from the input text and added to the agent's in-memory conversation array. If a token cache is present, a new turn marker is recorded in the cache.
behavior.database-persistence:
If a database connection and valid session identifier are present, the user message is persisted with kind "user". The persistence record includes JSON metadata containing the model name, temperature, and maximum completion token count drawn from the current configuration.
If persistence fails, a warning is logged and execution continues. The message already exists in memory and the agent proceeds to contact the provider regardless.
A failed database write does not prevent the LLM request from being sent. The in-memory message is the authoritative record; the database is an optional persistence layer at this stage.
behavior.response-state-clearing: The accumulated assistant response text from any previous exchange is discarded. The streaming line buffer is cleared. The tool iteration counter is reset to zero. This prepares the agent to receive a fresh response stream.
behavior.idle-flag-update: If a database connection is present, the agent's idle flag in the database is set to false, signaling that the agent is actively processing. Errors from this update are ignored.
behavior.state-transition: The agent transitions to the waiting-for-LLM state. This shows the activity spinner and hides the input buffer in the display.
behavior.provider-initialization:
The provider instance is obtained lazily: if a cached provider exists on the agent, it is reused. If not, a new provider is created based on the agent's configured provider name.
If provider creation fails (for example, due to missing credentials), the error message is appended to the scrollback, the agent transitions back to idle, and the function returns. No LLM request is sent.
behavior.request-construction: The LLM request is assembled from the agent's current conversation messages, system prompt, tool registry (filtered by any active toolset filter), and thinking level configuration. If request construction fails, the error is appended to the scrollback, the agent transitions to idle, and the function returns.
behavior.async-stream-start:
The provider's stream is started. Two callbacks are registered: one for incoming stream events (text deltas, tool call events, errors, done events), and one for completion. The function returns immediately; the provider operates asynchronously in the background.
A flag is set on the agent to signal the REPL event loop that an in-progress stream requires polling.
If stream start fails, the error is appended to the scrollback and the agent transitions to idle.
